goal: Audit the current VR-teleop configuration against ISO 10218-1 clause 5.7 (manual control mode) and separate sim-testable items from hardware-only items.
thoughts: Never cite ISO 10218-1 clause 5.7 subclauses from memory. teleop_safety_config inspects the current reduced-speed / enabling-device bindings in the scene. check_teleop_hardware inventories connected input devices. lookup_knowledge retrieves the actual clause text. The VR trigger is NOT a three-position enabling device — that specific topology (center=enable, released OR fully-pressed=stop) must live on the pendant hardware. Refuse to certify the teleop as safety-compliant.
code:
# Clause 5.7 audit. Row-by-row.

# 1. What safety parameters does the teleop bridge currently enforce?
teleop_safety_config(
    session_prim="<TELEOP_SESSION_PRIM>"
)

# 2. What input hardware is actually connected?
check_teleop_hardware(
    session_prim="<TELEOP_SESSION_PRIM>"
)

# 3. Pull ISO 10218-1 clause 5.7.1..5.7.5 — do NOT retype from memory
lookup_knowledge(query="ISO 10218-1 clause 5.7 manual control mode reduced speed enabling device three-position")

# Produce a checklist — one row per subclause:
#   Clause 5.7.1 ... | Testable in sim? | Current status | Action
#   Clause 5.7.2 ... | ...              | ...            | ...
#   Clause 5.7.3 (enabling device)   | HARDWARE ONLY  | MISSING        | install 3-pos device on pendant
#   Clause 5.7.4 (reduced speed)     | SIM TESTABLE   | NOT ENFORCED   | set TCP limit <=250 mm/s in teleop_safety_config
#   Clause 5.7.5 ... | ...              | ...            | ...
# What to tell Thomas:
#   - The VR trigger is NOT a three-position enabling device. Topology matters:
#     center=enable, released OR fully-pressed=stop. Must live on the pendant.
#   - Software-only speed limit is not a rated safety function under ISO 13849.
#   - This configuration is NOT safety-compliant and I will not say it is.
failure_modes: Accepting the VR trigger as an enabling device (wrong topology)
Treating software-only speed limit as a rated safety function
Citing clause 5.7 subclause numbers from memory instead of lookup_knowledge
Certifying the teleop setup as safety-compliant
